    "business_context": "A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer notification windows, vehicle-class enforcement, and configurable stop duration.     "bounded_context": "Multiple bounded contexts identified: (1) Route Optimization Context — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers, stop ordering, and ETA computation. (4) Customer Notification Context — owns notification rules, delivery proximity windows, and customer communication channels. These contexts must integrate but each owns its own invariants.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shift", "definition": "A bounded work period for a driver, beginning when they clock in and ending when they clock out. A shift is the lifecycle boundary within which route assignment and recalculation occur." },
      { "term": "Zone", "definition": "A geographic partition of the service area. Each zone has its own configurable parameters (e.g., stop duration). Deliveries and drivers are associated with zones." },
      { "term": "Optimized Route", "definition": "An ordered sequence of delivery stops calculated by the system to minimize travel time/distance for a driver within their zone during their shift." },
      { "term": "Pending Delivery", "definition": "A delivery that has been created and is awaiting assignment to a driver's route. Only pending deliveries in a driver's zone are candidates for route assignment." },
      { "term": "Recalculation Trigger", "definition": "An event that invalidates the current optimized route and forces the system to recompute. Three defined triggers: new delivery added to zone, delivery marked as failed, driver manual deviation." },
      { "term": "Next 3 Stops Window", "definition": "A proximity threshold defined as the upcoming 3 delivery stops on a driver's active route. Entering this window triggers customer notification." },
      { "term": "Vehicle Class", "definition": "A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that constrains which deliveries a driver can be assigned. Determined by the driver's license and their vehicle." },
      { "term": "Licensed Vehicle Class", "definition": "The set of vehicle classes a driver is certified and authorized to operate. This is a property of the driver, not the vehicle currently assigned." },
      { "term": "Stop Duration", "definition": "The estimated time a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes globally but is overridable per zone by the operations team." },
      { "term": "Manual Deviation", "definition": "When a driver physically departs from the system-suggested route sequence, detected by the system (e.g., via GPS or the driver skipping a stop)." },
      { "term": "Failed Delivery", "definition": "A delivery that was attempted but could not be completed (e.g., recipient unavailable, access denied). Triggers route recalculation to remove it from the sequence." }
